title: Crispy Spinach and Ricotta Parcels
summary: These simple and delicious spinach and ricotta parcels are perfect for a picnic. Watching the video will make folding them much easier.

method.description: Grab a large pan and place over a medium heat. Wash the spinach well and add straight into the pan with a pinch of salt. Cook for about 2 mins until the spinach has wilted. Set aside to cool.
Roughly chop the spinach and basil and place into a large bowl. Grate in a clove of garlic and your parmesan before adding the ricotta and nutmeg. Mix well.
Mix the flour in a small bowl with 5 tbsp of water. Melt the butter in a small pan and set aside. Preheat your oven to 180°C.

Take a sheet of filo and lay it out flat with the short end towards you. Cut it in half. Brush the long edge of one half with a light layer of the flour and water mix. Fold the right hand corner at 90 degrees and seal against the edge with the flour mix. Repeat with the second half.
Repeat that step again until you have a triangle. Open up the triangle like a cone and add 2 tbsp of the spinach mix.
Continue to fold over the excess, sealing with a little of the flour mix, until you have a triangular parcel. Repeat until you have used up all of the mix, which should make 8 parcels.
Place the parcels onto a baking tray and brush all over with the butter. Place into the oven for 15 to 20 mins until golden brown and crispy.

chefs.name: Jordon Ezra King
chefs.summary: Jordon Ezra King is a chef and storyteller. Having cooked, temped, and staged his way through a host of kitchens around the world, Jordon has now set his sights on sharing his knowledge with the world, using his platform to teach people how to work their way around a kitchen and educate them about the provenance of the dishes they're cooking while he does it.
